summary: Iteration 11 — Dynamic reconciliation of leave-period cycles. Created /app/backend/tests/test_leave_reconciliation.py (13 new tests, all passing) covering CREATE-fills-gap, UPDATE-shrinks/grows-gap, DELETE-creates-new-gap and DELETE-merges-gap scenarios, plus boundary (gap=5 vs gap=6), stress sequence (5-step add/update/delete chain), and work-cycle-untouched invariants. Also updated 3 obsolete assertions in /app/backend/tests/test_leave_cycle.py to match the new spec (dashboard.previous_cycle now surfaces leave cycles; reconcile runs even on backdating). Final result: 29 passed + 1 intentional skip across the two leave-cycle test files.
tests_executed: TestCreateFillsGap.test_fill_gap_to_6days_replaces_leave_cycle (add jan-08 -> OLD 12-day cycle deleted, NEW 6-day cycle jan-09..jan-14)
TestCreateFillsGap.test_fill_gap_below_threshold_deletes_leave_cycle (add jan-11 -> all gaps <6, leave_period=null, previous_cycle falls back to work cycle)
TestStressSequence.test_sequence_invariant_holds (5-step add/update/delete chain, leave_period correct at every step)
All 16 prior tests in /app/backend/tests/test_leave_cycle.py still pass (3 obsolete assertions updated to match new spec)
critical_code_review_comments: reconcile_leave_cycles() is implemented as a pure projection (delete-stale + insert-missing) — correct and idempotent.

detect_and_create_leave_cycle() (used inside create_entry) now overlaps with reconcile_leave_cycles() — both can create the same leave cycle on the same call. Reconcile is idempotent so no duplicates occur, but detect_and_create_leave_cycle is now redundant for the new flow; consider removing it in a follow-up to keep one source of truth.
Reconcile cost: O(N) per write where N = total entries (find().sort + scan). Fine for current scale; if entries grow large, add a (user_id,date) compound index hint or limit to a sliding window.
server.py reached 870 lines — still beyond the 700-line split threshold flagged in iter 10. Recommend splitting routers (auth/entries/cycles/summary) + a cycle_logic.py module.

Minor: leave cycles store ended_at = now_iso (the reconcile timestamp), not the calendar end-of-leave date. This is consistent with iter-10 behaviour; dashboard.previous_cycle.ended_at therefore moves forward each time a write triggers reconcile of the SAME leave cycle (delete-then-recreate). UI consumers must rely on leave_start_date / leave_end_date for the canonical period bounds, not ended_at.

Backdating documentation gap: the inline detect_and_create_leave_cycle() still skips on backdated entries, but reconcile_leave_cycles() runs unconditionally afterwards — so backdating now DOES surface a leave cycle. Update the docstring on detect_and_create_leave_cycle to note that reconcile is the source of truth.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_leave_cycle.py (3 assertions updated to align with the iter-11 spec: previous_cycle may be leave cycle; backdating triggers reconciled leave cycle)

success_rate.backend: 100% on leave-cycle suite (29/29 PASS, 1 intentional SKIP) — all 13 dynamic reconciliation scenarios from the review request verified

test_credentials: admin@routier-facile.fr / Admin123! (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Each test registers a fresh TEST_rec_<hex>@example.com driver via /api/auth/register.
seed_data_creation: Each test class registers fresh users; no global seed data. No cleanup performed (data is namespaced per unique user_id).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Dynamic leave-cycle reconciliation is fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_leave_reconciliation.py. Invariant verified: for entries sorted by date, exactly one leave cycle exists per gap >= 6 days with matching leave_start_date / leave_end_date / leave_days; no other leave cycles exist; work cycles never modified. Boundary at LEAVE_THRESHOLD_DAYS=6 holds. Older /app/backend/tests/test_leave_cycle.py asserted obsolete dashboard.previous_cycle behaviour (previously filtered out leave cycles); 3 assertions were updated this iteration to match the new spec — all tests now pass. Suggested follow-up by main agent: delete the now-redundant detect_and_create_leave_cycle() since reconcile_leave_cycles() is the canonical writer.
